commits: a3cf873d: test(0.8.12 Slice A): RED — rebuilt projection re-surfaces invalidated edge
83e0d686: fix(0.8.12 Slice A): t_invalid filter in FTS/vec projection SQL — rebuild-durable exclusion
findings_addressed: R-CON-2 named default-ON blocker (Slice-20 codex §9 [P2]): rebuild_projections() re-materialized an invalidated (non-superseded) edge's FTS/vec projection shadows because the rebuild SQL filtered only superseded_at, not t_invalid, while graph traversal already excludes t_invalid-ended edges via `t_invalid IS NULL OR datetime(t_invalid) > datetime('now')`.
additional_changes_made_in_scope: Added the same t_invalid recency filter to next_pending_projection_jobs's canonical_edges/'edge_fact' vec queue arm (fix site 2) — required for the vec surface to stay durable, not just FTS.

Added the same t_invalid recency filter to database_has_pending_projection_work's edge pending-work probe (fix site 3) — REQUIRED per the mandate: without this mirror, an invalidated-only edge that the (now-fixed) projection queue correctly skips would never gain a _fathomdb_projection_terminal row, so this probe would flag it as phantom-pending forever and drain()/wait_for_idle would hang. Verified this is load-bearing: the RED-test scenario with only fix sites 1+2 applied (not 3) would hang on the post-rebuild drain() call rather than merely fail an assertion.
Updated the KNOWN LIMITATION doc comment on prune_edge_projection_shadows to state the durable fix now lands here (was previously 'tracked as a follow-up, out of Slice-15 scope').
agent_verify_result: pass — RED confirmed at baseline (bec69825, before SQL edits): `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator --test rebuild_projections` failed with `assertion left == right failed: rebuild must NOT re-surface the t_invalid-excluded edge in search_index_edges — left: 1, right: 0` (exit 1). GREEN after the fix commit: same command exit 0, 3 passed (including new test slice_a_rebuild_projections_excludes_recency_invalidated_edge, which drives the REAL consolidate_with_provider recency path via the deterministic stub harness, not a direct t_invalid poke). `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test consolidation_value_test` exit 0, 1 passed. `cargo check -p fathomdb-engine --features operator` exit 0. `cargo check -p fathomdb-engine` (default features) exit 0. The full `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator` (all ~85 test binaries in the crate, most unrelated to this slice) exceeded a 2-minute foreground timeout and was still running with near-zero CPU after being backgrounded for several more minutes (likely a legitimately long/blocking test elsewhere in the crate, unrelated to the SQL touched here) — per the prompt's explicit allowance ('if it is very long, you may target the projection/rebuild tests and note scope'), it was killed and replaced with a scoped run of the 14 test binaries most load-bearing for this change: rebuild_projections, rebuild_vec0, rebuild_report, consolidate_provider, consolidation_value_test, excise_source, projection_runtime, slice20_graph_traversal (graph-traversal t_invalid predicate — the mirrored source of truth), pr_g12_recency, slice15_byo_llm_ingest (edge FTS/vec projection), batch_write_per_row_cursor, cursors, cursor_read_after_write, op_store. That scoped run: exit 0, 94 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored (a pre-existing #[ignore]'d test unrelated to this change) across all 14 binaries.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Cherry-pick both commits (a3cf873d test, 83e0d686 fix) onto the mainline 0.8.12 release branch. All edits are confined to src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/{src/lib.rs, tests/rebuild_projections.rs} plus this output.json — no cross-worktree or build-system touches. Recommend codex review confirm: (a) the three fix-site SQL predicates are byte-identical in form to the canonical graph-traversal predicate at lib.rs ~6949-6956; (b) fix site 3 (database_has_pending_projection_work) is genuinely load-bearing and not incidental scope creep — it is, per the reasoning captured in additional_changes_made_in_scope above and the inline comment added at that call site.
